outcome_text: Executive leadership of the organization takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment.
scoring_text: Knowledge of executive-level AI risk governance, including board and C-suite accountability structures, executive decision-making frameworks for AI risk acceptance, and leadership responsibility models for AI system development and deployment decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of executive-level AI risk governance, including board and C-suite accountability structures, executive decision-making frameworks for AI risk acceptance, and leadership responsibility models for AI system development and deployment decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-2.3 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.724, cosine: 0.869). Secondary: LS-K-010 (LS, cosine: 0.755), AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.734).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.2/10 (raw 0.724). Shared vocabulary: [accountability, ai, deployment, governance, knowledge, risk, structures]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [acceptance, board, c, decision, decisions, development] vs WIDAI terms [assignment, conditions, enable, lifecycle, management, model] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.909 (P=0.901, R=0.918). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.869 > STS 0.724 (gap +0.145). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [accountability, ai, deployment, governance, knowledge] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 25.6%. Shared tokens: [accountability, ai, deployment, governance, knowledge, risk, structures]. NIST AI RMF-only: [acceptance, board, c, decision, decisions, development]. WIDAI-only: [assignment, conditions, enable, lifecycle, management, model].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.145 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
